conventions.convention: All stored procedures use the prefix sp_ followed by domain_action. Example: sp_auth_login, sp_application_approve.
Procedures that touch more than one table MUST be wrapped in a BEGIN ... COMMIT / ROLLBACK block. The caller never commits manually.
Every CREATE / UPDATE / DELETE / status-change writes one row to audit_log via the sp_audit_write helper. Never skip it.
All ENUM transitions are validated inside the stored procedure. The application layer sends the desired new status; the procedure rejects invalid transitions with SIGNAL SQLSTATE '45000'.
Nullable columns are always returned as SQL NULL. The service layer maps NULL → null (JSON). Never coerce to "".
Slugs are generated in the SvelteKit server layer before calling the INSERT procedure, using: title.toLowerCase().replace(/\\s+/g,'-') .replace(/[^a-z0-9-]/g,'').substring(0,250) + '-' + Date.now()
Passwords are hashed with bcrypt (cost 12) in the SvelteKit service layer BEFORE passing to any stored procedure. Never pass plaintext.
Access tokens: HS256, 15 min TTL, payload { sub, role, userId }. Refresh tokens: stored as SHA-256 hash in refresh_tokens.token_hash. Token rotation on every /auth/refresh call.

procedure.name: sp_audit_write
procedure.description: Inserts a single row into audit_log. Called by all other procedures that modify data. Never called directly by the application layer.
procedure.sql: CREATE PROCEDURE sp_audit_write( IN p_actor_type ENUM('administrator','system'), IN p_actor_id INT UNSIGNED, IN p_action VARCHAR(50), IN p_entity_type VARCHAR(100), IN p_entity_id INT UNSIGNED, IN p_prev_value JSON, IN p_new_value JSON, IN p_description VARCHAR(500), IN p_ip_address VARCHAR(45) ) BEGIN INSERT INTO audit_log ( actor_type, actor_id, action, entity_type, entity_id, previous_value, new_value, description, ip_address, created_at ) VALUES ( p_actor_type, p_actor_id, p_action, p_entity_type, p_entity_id, p_prev_value, p_new_value, p_description, p_ip_address, NOW() ); END;

procedure.name: sp_collaborator_set_status
procedure.description:
Updates trajectory_status on a collaborator row, enforcing the allowed transition machine. Writes an audit entry automatically. Called internally by other procedures — never directly by the API.
Allowed transitions: nuevo → en_revision | inactivo en_revision → contactado | inactivo contactado → vinculado | inactivo vinculado → inactivo inactivo → en_revision (re-activation path)

procedure.name: sp_auth_store_refresh_token
procedure.description: Inserts a new refresh token record. Called after any successful login or token rotation. Old tokens for the same user+device should be revoked by the service layer before calling this.

param.name: p_token_hash
param.note: SHA-256 of the raw refresh token

procedure.returns: p_token_id INT UNSIGNED — the new refresh_tokens.id
procedure.sql: CREATE PROCEDURE sp_auth_store_refresh_token( IN p_user_type ENUM('administrator','collaborator'), IN p_user_id INT UNSIGNED, IN p_token_hash VARCHAR(255), IN p_expires_at TIMESTAMP, IN p_user_agent VARCHAR(512), IN p_ip_address VARCHAR(45), OUT p_token_id INT UNSIGNED ) BEGIN INSERT INTO refresh_tokens ( user_type, user_id, token_hash, expires_at, is_revoked, issued_at, user_agent, ip_address ) VALUES ( p_user_type, p_user_id, p_token_hash, p_expires_at, FALSE, NOW(), p_user_agent, p_ip_address ); SET p_token_id = LAST_INSERT_ID(); END;

procedure.name: sp_auth_revoke_token
procedure.description: Revokes a single refresh token by its hash. Used on logout and token rotation. Does not error if token not found (idempotent).

procedure.name: sp_auth_revoke_all_user_tokens
procedure.description: Revokes ALL active refresh tokens for a given user. Used on password change and admin deactivation.
procedure.sql: CREATE PROCEDURE sp_auth_revoke_all_user_tokens( IN p_user_type ENUM('administrator','collaborator'), IN p_user_id INT UNSIGNED ) BEGIN UPDATE refresh_tokens SET is_revoked = TRUE WHERE user_type = p_user_type AND user_id = p_user_id AND is_revoked = FALSE; END;

procedure.name: sp_auth_validate_refresh_token
procedure.description: Validates a refresh token by hash. Returns the token record only if it exists, is not revoked, and has not expired. Returns empty result set if invalid — the service layer must treat empty result as 401.
procedure.returns: Single row: id, user_type, user_id, expires_at, issued_at
procedure.sql: CREATE PROCEDURE sp_auth_validate_refresh_token(IN p_token_hash VARCHAR(255)) BEGIN SELECT id, user_type, user_id, expires_at, issued_at FROM refresh_tokens WHERE token_hash = p_token_hash AND is_revoked = FALSE AND expires_at > NOW(); END;

procedure.name: sp_auth_set_password
procedure.description: Sets or updates the password_hash for a collaborator. Used during account activation and password reset flows. Does NOT validate the old password — that must be done in the service layer first. Revokes all existing refresh tokens on completion (force re-login).

procedure.name: sp_collaborator_register
procedure.description: Inserts a new collaborator row from the public interest form. password_hash is NULL at this stage — set only after admin approval via the activation flow. Also inserts availability_slots rows and collaborator_tags rows in the same transaction. Triggers: service layer sends admin-new-signup email after commit.

param.name: p_tag_ids
param.note: JSON array of tag IDs e.g. [1,2,3]

param.name: p_availability_slots
param.note: JSON array of {day_of_week, time_from, time_to, notes}

procedure.name: sp_collaborator_update_profile
procedure.description:
Updates editable profile fields for a collaborator. Only fields passed as non-NULL are written (all-or-nothing per call — the service layer passes current values for unchanged fields). profile_complete is re-evaluated automatically.
profile_complete = TRUE when: major, current_university_year, expected_graduation_year, motivation_description are all non-null and non-empty.

procedure.name: sp_collaborator_sync_tags
procedure.description: Replaces all collaborator_tags rows for a collaborator with the provided set of tag IDs. Deletes removed tags, inserts new ones. Used on profile update when interest tags change.

procedure.name: sp_collaborator_sync_availability
procedure.description: Replaces all availability_slots rows for a collaborator. Full replacement strategy (delete all, re-insert).

procedure.name: sp_application_submit
procedure.description: Creates a new application record. Enforces: - Collaborator must have trajectory_status IN ('contactado','vinculado'). - No existing non-withdrawn application for the same project. - Project must be status='activo' and is_visible=TRUE. - If max_collaborators is set, current_collaborator_count must be less.

signal.condition: Collaborator status does not permit application

procedure.name: sp_application_set_status
procedure.description:
Moves an application to en_revision or rechazada. Does NOT handle approval — use sp_application_approve for that.
Allowed transitions: pendiente → en_revision | rechazada en_revision → rechazada pendiente → retirada (collaborator self-withdraw only)

procedure.name: sp_application_approve
procedure.description:
*** CORE TRANSACTIONAL PROCEDURE ***
Atomically approves an application AND creates the resulting assignment in a single database transaction. Also: 1. Sets application.status = 'aprobada' 2. Sets application.approved_at = NOW() 3. Inserts into assignments 4. Increments projects.current_collaborator_count 5. Sets collaborator.trajectory_status = 'vinculado' (only if not already vinculado) 6. Writes 2 audit_log entries (approve + assign)
After commit, the service layer sends the activation email via Resend.
Error if: - Application not in ('pendiente','en_revision') - Collaborator already has an active assignment for this project - Project at max capacity

procedure.name: sp_assignment_create_manual
procedure.description: Creates an assignment directly (without an application). Used for admin-initiated linkages. Also auto-promotes collaborator to trajectory_status = 'vinculado' and increments project counter.

procedure.name: sp_assignment_end
procedure.description:
Ends an active assignment. Sets ended_at, end_reason, and status. Decrements projects.current_collaborator_count.
If the collaborator has NO remaining active assignments after this, trajectory_status is demoted to 'inactivo'.
Allowed end statuses: finalizado | removido | pausado

procedure.name: sp_project_create
procedure.description: Creates a new project with tags and meeting_days in one transaction. Slug is generated in the service layer and passed in.

procedure.name: sp_project_set_status
procedure.description:
Updates project status with transition enforcement.
Allowed transitions: proximo → activo | archivado activo → en_pausa | completado | archivado en_pausa → activo | archivado completado → archivado

procedure.name: sp_project_toggle_visibility
procedure.description: Toggles is_visible for a project. Simple boolean flip.

procedure.name: sp_event_create
procedure.description: Creates a new event with tags and managers in one transaction. Mirrors the project creation pattern.

procedure.name: sp_event_set_status
procedure.description:
Sets event status with transition enforcement.
Allowed transitions: proximo → abierto | cancelado abierto → en_curso | cancelado en_curso → finalizado

procedure.name: sp_tag_create
procedure.description: Creates a new tag. System tags (is_system=TRUE) can only be created at seed time.

procedure.name: sp_tag_delete
procedure.description: Deletes a tag and removes all junction references. System tags (is_system = TRUE) cannot be deleted.

procedure.name: sp_content_upsert_home_hero
procedure.description: Deactivates all existing home_hero rows, then inserts or updates the given row. Only one hero is active at a time.

procedure.name: sp_content_update_generic
procedure.description: Generic UPDATE for simple single-active content entities: dlab_identity, contact_info, interest_form_config. Entity-specific fields are passed as a JSON payload and applied in the service layer via Drizzle ORM. This procedure handles only the updated_by_admin_id / updated_at stamp and audit write. Use entity-specific service functions for full field updates.
procedure.note: Full field updates for these entities are handled in the Drizzle ORM service layer using strongly-typed update objects. This procedure only records the audit trail.
procedure.sql: -- Audit-only procedure called after Drizzle ORM update CREATE PROCEDURE sp_content_audit_update( IN p_table_name VARCHAR(100), IN p_entity_id INT UNSIGNED, IN p_admin_id INT UNSIGNED ) BEGIN CALL sp_audit_write( 'administrator', p_admin_id, 'update', p_table_name, p_entity_id, NULL, NULL, CONCAT(p_table_name, ' content updated by admin ', p_admin_id), NULL ); END;

procedure.name: sp_value_proposition_reorder
procedure.description: Updates sort_order for multiple value_propositions rows. Takes a JSON array of {id, sort_order} pairs.

procedure.name: sp_media_register
procedure.description: Inserts a media_assets record after file upload to Docker volume. Called by the service layer once the file is physically saved.
procedure.sql: CREATE PROCEDURE sp_media_register( IN p_original_filename VARCHAR(500), IN p_stored_filename VARCHAR(500), IN p_public_url VARCHAR(1000), IN p_storage_type VARCHAR(10), IN p_storage_path VARCHAR(1000), IN p_mime_type VARCHAR(100), IN p_file_size_bytes INT UNSIGNED, IN p_entity_type VARCHAR(100), IN p_entity_id INT UNSIGNED, IN p_alt_text VARCHAR(300), IN p_caption VARCHAR(500), IN p_uploaded_by_admin_id INT UNSIGNED, OUT p_media_id INT UNSIGNED ) BEGIN INSERT INTO media_assets ( original_filename, stored_filename, public_url, storage_type, storage_path, mime_type, file_size_bytes, entity_type, entity_id, alt_text, caption, uploaded_by_admin_id, created_at ) VALUES ( p_original_filename, p_stored_filename, p_public_url, p_storage_type, p_storage_path, p_mime_type, p_file_size_bytes, p_entity_type, p_entity_id, p_alt_text, p_caption, p_uploaded_by_admin_id, NOW() ); SET p_media_id = LAST_INSERT_ID(); END;

procedure.name: sp_media_delete
procedure.description: Deletes a media_assets record. Physical file deletion is handled in the service layer BEFORE calling this procedure. Rejects deletion if the media URL is still referenced by any content entity (hard guard against orphan URLs).

view.name: vw_public_projects
view.description: Projects visible on the public portal. Used by the public /projects route. Pre-joins tags as a JSON array for single-query card rendering.
view.sql: CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW vw_public_projects AS SELECT p.id, p.title, p.slug, p.short_description, p.full_description, p.target_audience, p.required_skills, p.participation_mode, p.header_image_url, p.video_url, p.status, p.is_highlighted, p.max_collaborators, p.current_collaborator_count, JSON_ARRAYAGG( JSON_OBJECT('id', t.id, 'name', t.name, 'slug', t.slug, 'category', t.category) ) AS tags, GROUP_CONCAT( DISTINCT CONCAT(pmd.day_of_week, COALESCE(CONCAT(' (', pmd.notes, ')'), '')) ORDER BY pmd.day_of_week SEPARATOR ', ' ) AS meeting_days_summary FROM projects p LEFT JOIN project_tags pt ON pt.project_id = p.id LEFT JOIN tags t ON t.id = pt.tag_id LEFT JOIN project_meeting_days pmd ON pmd.project_id = p.id WHERE p.is_visible = TRUE AND p.status NOT IN ('archivado') GROUP BY p.id;

view.name: vw_public_events
view.sql: CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW vw_public_events AS SELECT e.id, e.title, e.slug, e.type, e.short_description, e.full_description, e.target_audience, e.location, e.event_date, e.event_end_date, e.registration_deadline, e.capacity, e.header_image_url, e.banner_image_url, e.registration_url, e.status, e.is_highlighted, JSON_ARRAYAGG( IF(t.id IS NOT NULL, JSON_OBJECT('id', t.id, 'name', t.name, 'slug', t.slug), NULL) ) AS tags FROM events e LEFT JOIN event_tags et ON et.event_id = e.id LEFT JOIN tags t ON t.id = et.tag_id WHERE e.is_visible = TRUE AND e.status NOT IN ('cancelado') GROUP BY e.id;

view.name: vw_admin_collaborators
view.description: Full collaborator list for admin panel with tag list and active assignment count pre-computed.
view.sql: CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW vw_admin_collaborators AS SELECT c.id, c.first_name, c.middle_name, c.last_name, c.second_last_name, c.personal_email, c.usfq_email, c.phone_number, c.date_of_birth, c.major, c.current_university_year, c.expected_graduation_year, c.experience_description, c.motivation_description, c.interest_in_training, c.interest_in_research, c.interest_in_fabrication, c.trajectory_status, c.profile_complete, c.intake_source, c.created_at, c.updated_at, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM assignments a WHERE a.collaborator_id = c.id AND a.status = 'activo') AS active_assignment_count, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM applications ap WHERE ap.collaborator_id = c.id AND ap.status = 'pendiente') AS pending_application_count, JSON_ARRAYAGG( IF(t.id IS NOT NULL, JSON_OBJECT('id', t.id, 'name', t.name, 'category', t.category), NULL) ) AS tags FROM collaborators c LEFT JOIN collaborator_tags ct ON ct.collaborator_id = c.id LEFT JOIN tags t ON t.id = ct.tag_id GROUP BY c.id;

view.name: vw_collaborator_match_score
view.description: Matching view: for each (collaborator, project) pair computes a tag_overlap_count (shared tags) and a schedule_overlap flag (TRUE if at least one availability_slot overlaps a project_meeting_day). Used by the admin linkage suggestion feature.
view.sql: CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW vw_collaborator_match_score AS SELECT c.id AS collaborator_id, p.id AS project_id, p.title AS project_title, c.trajectory_status, COUNT(DISTINCT shared.tag_id) AS tag_overlap_count, MAX( CASE WHEN asl.day_of_week IS NOT NULL AND pmd.day_of_week IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END ) AS has_schedule_overlap FROM collaborators c CROSS JOIN projects p LEFT JOIN collaborator_tags ct ON ct.collaborator_id = c.id LEFT JOIN project_tags ptag ON ptag.project_id = p.id AND ptag.tag_id = ct.tag_id LEFT JOIN tags shared ON shared.id = ct.tag_id AND shared.id = ptag.tag_id LEFT JOIN availability_slots asl ON asl.collaborator_id = c.id LEFT JOIN project_meeting_days pmd ON pmd.project_id = p.id AND pmd.day_of_week = asl.day_of_week WHERE p.is_visible = TRUE AND p.status = 'activo' GROUP BY c.id, p.id;

steps.step: Query administrators WHERE usfq_email = email AND is_active = TRUE
Sign JWT access token {sub: id, role:'admin', userId: id}, 15min
Guard: password_hash IS NOT NULL → 403 'Account not yet activated'
Sign JWT {sub: id, role:'collaborator', userId: id}
Issue new access + refresh token pair

method.name: sendActivationEmail
method.description: Called after sp_application_approve commits. Generates a signed activation URL token (JWT, 72h TTL, payload: {purpose:'activate', collaboratorId}). Sends via Resend template 'collaborator-approved-activate'. Token is NOT stored in DB — it is verified by JWT signature alone.

steps.step: Verify activation JWT — check purpose='activate', not expired
Validate password strength (min 8 chars, 1 upper, 1 digit)
Return 200 with new login token pair

method.name: approveApplication

steps.step: CALL sp_application_approve(appId, adminId, role, notes, ip) → assignmentId
Query collaborator email from collaborators WHERE id = collaboratorId

method.error-handling: Catch SQLSTATE 45020/45021/45022 → map to HTTP 409 Conflict.

method.name: getSuggestionsForProject
method.description: Queries vw_collaborator_match_score WHERE project_id = id AND trajectory_status IN ('contactado','vinculado') AND tag_overlap_count > 0 ORDER BY tag_overlap_count DESC, has_schedule_overlap DESC LIMIT 10. Returns suggestions with collaborator profile snippets.

method.name: getSuggestionsForCollaborator
method.description: Queries vw_collaborator_match_score WHERE collaborator_id = id ORDER BY tag_overlap_count DESC, has_schedule_overlap DESC Returns top 5 matching active projects.

method.name: getPublicHomePage
method.description: Parallel queries via Promise.all: [0] SELECT * FROM home_hero WHERE is_active = TRUE LIMIT 1 [1] SELECT * FROM dlab_identity WHERE is_active = TRUE LIMIT 1 [2] SELECT * FROM value_propositions WHERE is_visible = TRUE ORDER BY sort_order [3] SELECT * FROM participation_steps WHERE is_visible = TRUE ORDER BY step_number [4] SELECT * FROM contact_info WHERE is_active = TRUE LIMIT 1 [5] SELECT * FROM social_links WHERE is_visible = TRUE ORDER BY sort_order [6] SELECT id,title,slug,short_description,header_image_url,status FROM vw_public_projects WHERE is_highlighted = TRUE LIMIT 3 [7] SELECT id,title,slug,short_description,header_image_url,event_date,type FROM vw_public_events WHERE is_highlighted = TRUE LIMIT 3 Returns unified JSON for SvelteKit load function cache (SWR 5min).

error.meaning: Generic invalid transition / business rule violation
Collaborator status does not permit action
Application cannot be approved from current status
Media asset in use by content entity
